1711.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sakhr al-Ghamidi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Allah, bless my nation in their early mornings.' And when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent out an expedition, he would send it at the beginning of the day. This man was a merchant and he would send his workers out at the beginning of the day, and his wealth grew.
1711.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Shakhr al-Ghamidi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ya Allah, berkahilah umatku di waktu pagi mereka.' Dan ketika Rasulullah ﷺ mengirim pasukan ekspedisi, beliau mengirimnya di awal hari. Laki-laki ini adalah seorang pedagang dan dia mengirim para pekerjanya di awal hari, dan hartanya pun bertambah banyak.

1712.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b (ibn Malik, may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ rarely departed when he intended a journey except on Thursday.
1712.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b (ibn Malik, radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ jarang keluar ketika hendak bepergian kecuali pada hari Kamis.

1713.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with them) from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ who said: 'The best of companions in the sight of Allah is the one who is best to his companion, and the best of neighbors in the sight of Allah is the one who is best to his neighbor.'
1713.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-'Ash (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) dari Rasulullah ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik teman dalam pandangan Allah adalah yang paling baik kepada temannya, dan sebaik-baik tetangga dalam pandangan Allah adalah yang paling baik kepada tetangganya.'

1714.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The best number of companions is four, and the best army is four thousand, and the best detachment is four hundred. An army of twelve thousand will never be defeated out of smallness of numbers if they remain patient and sincere.'
1714.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik jumlah teman adalah empat, dan sebaik-baik pasukan adalah empat ribu, dan sebaik-baik detasemen adalah empat ratus. Pasukan yang berjumlah dua belas ribu tidak akan pernah dikalahkan karena sedikitnya jumlah jika mereka tetap sabar dan jujur.'

1715.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ appointed a man over an expedition, he would advise him privately to fear Allah and to treat the Muslims with him well, and say: 'Fight in the name of Allah and in the cause of Allah. Fight those who disbelieve in Allah. Fight and do not break pledges, do not take spoils unlawfully, do not mutilate, and do not kill a child.'
1715.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ mengangkat seseorang atas suatu ekspedisi, beliau menasihatinya secara pribadi untuk bertakwa kepada Allah dan memperlakukan kaum Muslimin yang bersamanya dengan baik, dan bersabda: 'Berperanglah dengan nama Allah dan di jalan Allah. Perangilah mereka yang kafir kepada Allah. Berperanglah dan jangan mengingkari janji, jangan mengambil rampasan secara tidak sah, jangan mencincang, dan jangan membunuh anak-anak.'

1716.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not wish to meet the enemy, and ask Allah for well-being. But if you do meet them, stand firm and increase your remembrance of Allah. And if they charge noisily, keep silent.'
1716.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan berangan-angan bertemu musuh, dan mintalah kepada Allah keselamatan. Namun jika kamu memang bertemu mereka, teguhkan diri dan perbanyak mengingat Allah. Dan jika mereka menyerang dengan gaduh, berdiam dirilah.'

1717.iwh_en: It was narrated from Suhayb (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to supplicate during the days of Hunayn: 'O Allah, by You I strive, by You I charge, and by You I fight.'
1717.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Suhaib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berdoa pada hari-hari Perang Hunain: 'Ya Allah, dengan-Mu aku berjuang, dengan-Mu aku menyerbu, dan dengan-Mu aku berperang.'

1718.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraydah (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ appointed a man over an expedition, he would advise him: 'When you meet your enemy from the polytheists, call them to one of three things: call them to Islam, and if they respond then accept it and refrain; then call them to move from their land to the land of the emigrants... If they refuse to enter Islam, ask them to pay the jizyah; if they do, accept it and refrain; if they refuse, seek the help of Allah and fight them...'
1718.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ mengangkat seseorang atas suatu ekspedisi, beliau menasihatinya: 'Ketika kamu bertemu musuhmu dari kaum musyrikin, ajaklah mereka kepada salah satu dari tiga hal: ajaklah mereka kepada Islam, jika mereka menerima maka terimalah dan tahanlah diri; kemudian ajaklah mereka pindah dari tanah mereka ke tanah kaum muhajirin... Jika mereka menolak masuk Islam, mintalah mereka membayar jizyah; jika mereka lakukan, terimalah dan tahanlah diri; jika mereka menolak, mintalah pertolongan Allah dan perangilah mereka...'

1719.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ never fought a people without first calling them to Islam.'
1719.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ tidak pernah memerangi suatu kaum tanpa terlebih dahulu mengajak mereka kepada Islam.'

1720.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ would raid at the time of the Fajr prayer, and would listen out: if he heard the adhan he would hold back, and if he did not hear the adhan he would raid.
1720.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa menyerang pada waktu shalat Fajar, dan beliau mendengarkan: jika mendengar adzan beliau menahan diri, dan jika tidak mendengar adzan beliau menyerang.

1721.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aws ibn Abi Aws al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the delegation of Thaqif... A man came and whispered to him, and he said: 'Go and kill him.' Then he said: 'Does he not testify that there is no god but Allah?' They said: Yes. He said: 'I was commanded to fight people until they say: there is no god but Allah; when they say it, their blood and wealth are inviolable to me except by right.'
1721.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aws ibn Abi Aws al-Tsaqafi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Aku datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dalam delegasi Tsaqif... Seorang laki-laki datang dan berbisik kepadanya, dan beliau bersabda: 'Pergi dan bunuh dia.' Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Apakah dia tidak bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah?' Mereka berkata: Ya. Beliau bersabda: 'Aku diperintahkan untuk memerangi manusia hingga mereka mengucapkan: tidak ada tuhan selain Allah; ketika mereka mengucapkannya, darah dan harta mereka terlindungi dariku kecuali dengan alasan yang benar.'

1722.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The blood of a man who testifies that there is no god but Allah is not lawful except in one of three cases: a life for a life, the married adulterer, and the one who abandons his religion and separates from the community.'
1722.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud, radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Darah seorang laki-laki yang bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah tidak halal kecuali dalam salah satu dari tiga kasus: jiwa dibalas jiwa, pezina yang sudah menikah, dan orang yang meninggalkan agamanya dan memisahkan diri dari jamaah.'

1723.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent the Army of Commanders, and they set out. They were away as long as Allah willed, then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ascended the pulpit, commanded, and it was called out: 'Prayer is gathered!'
1723.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengirim Pasukan Para Komandan, dan mereka pun berangkat. Mereka pergi selama yang Allah kehendaki, kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ naik mimbar, memberi perintah, dan diumumkan: 'Shalat berjama'ah!'

1724.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'The one who is consulted is entrusted.'
1724.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Orang yang dimintai pendapat adalah orang yang dipercaya.'

1725.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he intended a campaign, would give a different impression about his real destination.
1725.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika hendak berperang, akan memberikan kesan berbeda tentang tujuan sebenarnya.

1726.iwh_en: It was narrated from Salamah ibn al-Akwa' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I dueled a man and killed him, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me his spoils.' And our battle cry with Khalid ibn al-Walid was: 'Amit' — meaning: kill.
1726.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Salamah ibn al-Akwa' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku bertarung satu lawan satu dengan seorang laki-laki dan membunuhnya, dan Rasulullah ﷺ memberiku rampasannya.' Dan seruan perang kami bersama Khalid ibn al-Walid adalah: 'Amit' — artinya: bunuh.

1727.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Fihri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the campaign of Hunayn on a very hot day... The Prophet ﷺ took a handful of dust and threw it at their faces saying: 'May the faces be disfigured!' And Allah defeated the polytheists. Yaʿla said: Their sons later narrated to me that their fathers said: There was not one of us who did not have both eyes and mouth filled with dust.
1727.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Fihri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam Perang Hunain pada hari yang sangat panas... Nabi ﷺ mengambil segenggam debu dan melemparkannya ke wajah mereka sambil bersabda: 'Semoga wajah-wajah itu menjadi jelek!' Dan Allah mengalahkan kaum musyrikin. Ya'la berkata: Putra-putra mereka kemudian menceritakan kepadaku bahwa ayah-ayah mereka berkata: Tidak ada seorang pun di antara kami yang kedua mata dan mulutnya tidak dipenuhi debu.

1728.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to us while we were with him in a gathering: 'Pledge allegiance to me that you will not associate anything with Allah, will not steal, will not commit adultery, will not kill your children, will not bring a slander you fabricate between your hands and feet — whoever fulfills this, his reward is with Allah; whoever commits any of these sins and Allah conceals it, his matter is with Allah: if He wills He will punish him, and if He wills He will pardon him; and whoever commits it and is punished for it in this world, that is his expiation.'
1728.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepada kami saat kami bersamanya dalam suatu majelis: 'Bai'atlah aku bahwa kalian tidak akan menyekutukan sesuatu pun dengan Allah, tidak mencuri, tidak berzina, tidak membunuh anak-anak kalian, tidak membawa kedustaan yang kalian ada-adakan antara tangan dan kaki kalian — barangsiapa memenuhi ini, pahalanya ada di sisi Allah; barangsiapa melakukan dosa-dosa ini dan Allah menyembunyikannya, urusannya ada di sisi Allah: jika Dia berkehendak Dia akan menghukumnya, dan jika Dia berkehendak Dia akan memaafkannya; dan barangsiapa melakukannya dan dihukum atasnya di dunia ini, itu adalah kafarah baginya.'

1729.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with them) that he said: 'On the day of Hudaybiyyah we were one thousand four hundred; we pledged allegiance to him while Umar was holding his hand under the tree — it was a samura tree. And we pledged not to flee, but we did not pledge to die.'
1729.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa dia berkata: 'Pada hari Hudaibiyyah kami berjumlah seribu empat ratus; kami berbaiat kepadanya sementara Umar memegang tangannya di bawah pohon — itu adalah pohon samura. Dan kami berjanji tidak akan lari, namun kami tidak berjanji untuk mati.'

1730.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' ibn Azib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was carrying earth with us on the Day of al-Ahzab, and the dust had covered the whiteness of his armpits, while he was saying: 'O Allah, were it not for You, we would not have been guided, nor given in charity, nor prayed. So send down tranquility upon us and make our feet firm if we meet them. The enemies have transgressed against us, and if they seek to cause strife, we refuse!' And he raised his voice with it.
1730.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' ibn Azib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ membawa tanah bersama kami pada Hari al-Ahzab, dan debu telah menutupi putihnya ketiak beliau, sementara beliau berkata: 'Ya Allah, kalau bukan karena Engkau, kami tidak akan mendapat petunjuk, tidak bersedekah, dan tidak shalat. Maka turunkanlah ketenangan kepada kami dan teguhkanlah kaki kami jika kami bertemu mereka. Para musuh telah melampaui batas terhadap kami, dan jika mereka ingin menimbulkan fitnah, kami menolak!' Dan beliau mengangkat suaranya dengan itu.

1731.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ entered Mecca in the year of the Conquest with a helmet on his head. When he took it off, a man came and said: O Messenger of Allah, Ibn Khatal is clinging to the curtains of the Ka'bah. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Kill him.'
1731.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memasuki Mekah pada tahun Fathu Makkah dengan mengenakan helm perang di kepalanya. Ketika beliau melepasnya, seorang laki-laki datang dan berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, Ibnu Khatal sedang bergantung pada tirai Ka'bah. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bunuh dia.'

1732.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the pommel of the Prophet's ﷺ sword was made of silver.
1732.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa gagang pedang Nabi ﷺ terbuat dari perak.

1733.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Talhah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, when he was victorious over a people, would like to stay in their encampment for three days.
1733.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Thalhah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ, ketika menang atas suatu kaum, lebih suka tinggal di perkampungan mereka selama tiga hari.

1734.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ burned the date palms of Banu al-Nadir.'
1734.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ membakar pohon-pohon kurma Banu al-Nadhir.'

1735.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent us in an expedition and said: 'If you capture so-and-so and so-and-so, burn them with fire.' Then the next day he sent to us and said: 'I had commanded you to burn these two men with fire, but then I saw that it is not befitting for anyone to punish with fire except Allah. So if you capture them, kill them.'
1735.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengirim kami dalam suatu ekspedisi dan bersabda: 'Jika kalian menangkap si fulan dan si fulan, bakarlah mereka dengan api.' Kemudian keesokan harinya beliau mengirim pesan kepada kami dan bersabda: 'Aku telah memerintahkan kalian untuk membakar dua orang ini dengan api, namun kemudian aku melihat bahwa tidak layak bagi siapa pun untuk menyiksa dengan api kecuali Allah. Jadi jika kalian menangkap mereka, bunuhlah mereka.'

1736.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that a woman was found killed in one of the expeditions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the killing of women and children.
1736.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa seorang wanita ditemukan terbunuh dalam salah satu ekspedisi Rasulullah ﷺ, dan Rasulullah ﷺ melarang pembunuhan wanita dan anak-anak.

1737.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Aswad ibn Sari' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: We went out with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a campaign and were victorious over the polytheists. The people rushed to kill until they killed the children. This reached the Prophet ﷺ and he said: 'What is wrong with people that they went so far in killing that they killed the children? Beware: never kill a child.' He said it three times.
1737.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Aswad ibn Sari' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Kami keluar bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam suatu pertempuran dan menang atas kaum musyrikin. Orang-orang bergegas membunuh hingga mereka membunuh anak-anak. Hal ini sampai kepada Nabi ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: 'Ada apa dengan orang-orang yang sedemikian jauh dalam membunuh hingga membunuh anak-anak? Ingatlah: jangan sekali-kali membunuh anak-anak.' Beliau mengatakannya tiga kali.

1738.iwh_en: Atiyyah al-Qurazi (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'We were presented before the Prophet ﷺ that day: whoever had grown pubic hair was killed, and whoever had not grown it was spared' — and I was among those who had not grown hair, so they did not kill me. That refers to the day of Banu Qurayzah.
1738.iwh_id: Atiyyah al-Qurazi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Kami dihadapkan kepada Nabi ﷺ hari itu: siapa yang telah tumbuh rambut kemaluan dibunuh, dan siapa yang belum tumbuh dibiarkan' — dan aku termasuk yang belum tumbuh rambut, sehingga mereka tidak membunuhku. Itu mengacu pada hari Banu Quraizhah.

1739.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Musa (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Free the captive, feed the hungry.'
1739.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Musa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Bebaskan tawanan, beri makan orang yang lapar.'

1740.iwh_en: It was narrated from Imran ibn Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ransomed one man for two men.
1740.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Imran ibn Husain (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menebus satu orang dengan dua orang.

1741.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'I was given five things not given to any prophet before me: I was sent to the red and the black; the earth was made a place of prostration and a means of purification for me; war spoils were made lawful for me, which was not lawful for anyone before me; I was helped by fear that strikes in the heart of the enemy at a distance of a month's journey; and I was told: Ask and it will be given. So I kept my supplication as intercession for my nation, and it will reach those among you who do not associate anything with Allah.'
1741.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dzar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku diberi lima hal yang tidak diberikan kepada nabi mana pun sebelumku: aku diutus kepada yang merah dan yang hitam; bumi dijadikan tempat sujud dan alat bersuci bagiku; rampasan perang dihalalkan bagiku, yang tidak dihalalkan bagi siapa pun sebelumku; aku ditolong dengan rasa takut yang melanda hati musuh sejauh perjalanan satu bulan; dan aku diberi tahu: Mintalah maka akan dikabulkan. Maka aku menyimpan doaku sebagai syafaat untuk umatku, dan itu akan menjangkau mereka di antara kalian yang tidak menyekutukan sesuatu pun dengan Allah.'

1742.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ distributed the spoils of Hunayn at al-Ji'rranah.
1742.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ membagi rampasan Hunain di al-Ji'rranah.

1743.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Abi Layla (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I witnessed the Conquest of Khaybar with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The polytheists were routed and we fell upon their belongings. The people rushed for the camels they found. Before long the pots were boiling, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ordered them overturned. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ distributed among us, giving one sheep for every ten men.'
1743.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Abi Layla (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku menyaksikan Penaklukan Khaibar bersama Rasulullah ﷺ. Kaum musyrikin melarikan diri dan kami jatuh ke harta benda mereka. Orang-orang bergegas kepada unta-unta yang mereka temukan. Sebelum lama, pot-pot sudah mendidih, dan Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan agar mereka dibalikkan. Kemudian Rasulullah ﷺ membagi di antara kami, memberi satu ekor kambing untuk setiap sepuluh orang.'

1744.iwh_en: Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) wrote to Najdah ibn Amir in reply to his questions: 'You asked about the share of the close relatives mentioned by Allah — we used to think that the relatives of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ were they, but our people refused it to us.'
1744.iwh_id: Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) menulis kepada Najdah ibn Amir sebagai balasan atas pertanyaannya: 'Kamu bertanya tentang bagian kerabat dekat yang disebutkan oleh Allah — kami dahulu mengira bahwa kerabat Rasulullah ﷺ adalah mereka, tetapi kaum kami menolaknya kepada kami.'

1745.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the day of Khaybar gave the horseman three shares and the foot soldier one share.
1745.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ pada hari Khaibar memberi penunggang kuda tiga bagian dan pejalan kaki satu bagian.

1746.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I never attended a distribution of war spoils with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ without receiving my share — except on the day of Khaybar, for it was exclusively for the people of Hudaybiyyah. Abu Musa and Abu Hurairah had arrived between Hudaybiyyah and Khaybar.'
1746.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah menghadiri pembagian rampasan perang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ tanpa menerima bagianku — kecuali pada hari Khaibar, karena itu khusus untuk orang-orang Hudaibiyyah. Abu Musa dan Abu Hurairah tiba di antara Hudaibiyyah dan Khaibar.'

1747.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umayr, client of Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him), that he said: I witnessed Khaybar while I was an owned slave, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me some of the lesser goods and gave me a sword, saying: 'Wear this around your neck.'
1747.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umayr, bekas hamba sahaya milik Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa dia berkata: Aku menyaksikan Khaibar sementara aku adalah seorang budak yang dimiliki, dan Rasulullah ﷺ memberiku beberapa barang yang lebih rendah nilainya dan memberiku pedang, bersabda: 'Kenakan ini di lehermu.'

1748.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he forbade that shares of war spoils be sold before they are distributed.
1748.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau melarang bagian-bagian rampasan perang dijual sebelum dibagi.

1749.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ruwayfi' ibn Thabit al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he addressed the people and said: I only stand before you with what I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ who stood before us on the day of Khaybar when we conquered it and said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him not approach any captive woman until she has been declared clear of pregnancy.'
1749.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ruwayfi' ibn Tsabit al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkhutbah kepada orang-orang dan berkata: Aku hanya berdiri di hadapan kalian dengan apa yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ yang berdiri di hadapan kami pada hari Khaibar ketika kami menaklukkannya dan bersabda: 'Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, jangan mendekati wanita tawanan manapun hingga dia dinyatakan bersih dari kehamilan.'

1750.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ saw a pregnant woman at the entrance of a tent and said: 'Perhaps he approached her?' They said: Yes. He said: 'I was about to curse him with a curse that would follow him into his grave — how would he inherit from her when it is not lawful for him, and how would he enslave her when it is not lawful for him.'
1750.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melihat seorang wanita hamil di pintu kemah dan bersabda: 'Mungkin dia telah mendekatinya?' Mereka berkata: Ya. Beliau bersabda: 'Aku hampir melaknatnya dengan laknat yang akan mengikutinya ke dalam kuburannya — bagaimana dia akan mewarisi darinya sementara itu tidak halal baginya, dan bagaimana dia akan memperbudaknya sementara itu tidak halal baginya.'

1751.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ayyub (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was in an army and the children were separated from their mothers. He saw them crying, and he began returning each child to its mother, saying: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: whoever separates a mother from her child, Allah will separate him from his loved ones on the Day of Resurrection.'
1751.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ayyub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berada dalam suatu pasukan dan anak-anak dipisahkan dari ibu-ibu mereka. Dia melihat mereka menangis, dan dia mulai mengembalikan setiap anak kepada ibunya, sambil berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: barangsiapa memisahkan ibu dari anaknya, Allah akan memisahkannya dari orang-orang yang dicintainya pada Hari Kiamat.'

1752.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sakhr ibn al-Aylah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I captured the paternal aunt of al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah and brought her to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and al-Mughirah asked the Prophet ﷺ about his aunt. He said: 'O Sakhr, when people enter Islam they protect their wealth and blood, so return her to him.' And there was a water source belonging to Banu Sulaym — they entered Islam and came to him asking about it, and the Messenger ﷺ called me and said: 'O Sakhr, when people enter Islam they protect their wealth and blood, so return it to them.' So I returned it.
1752.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Shakhr ibn al-Aylah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Aku menangkap bibi dari pihak ayah al-Mughirah ibn Syu'bah dan membawanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, dan al-Mughirah bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang bibinya. Beliau bersabda: 'Wahai Shakhr, ketika orang-orang masuk Islam mereka melindungi harta dan darah mereka, maka kembalikanlah dia kepadanya.' Dan ada sumber air milik Banu Sulaim — mereka masuk Islam dan datang kepadanya meminta tentang itu, dan Rasulullah ﷺ memanggilku dan bersabda: 'Wahai Shakhr, ketika orang-orang masuk Islam mereka melindungi harta dan darah mereka, maka kembalikanlah itu kepada mereka.' Maka aku mengembalikannya.

1753.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent out an expedition in which Ibn Umar was included, and they captured many camels. Their shares came to twelve camels each, or eleven camels, and they were given one extra camel each as a bonus.
1753.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengirim pasukan ekspedisi yang di dalamnya terdapat Ibnu Umar, dan mereka menangkap banyak unta. Bagian mereka masing-masing dua belas unta, atau sebelas unta, dan mereka diberi satu unta tambahan masing-masing sebagai bonus.

1754.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he raided into enemy territory, would grant a bonus of one quarter; and when he was returning and all the people were together, he would grant a bonus of one third.
1754.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika menyerbu ke wilayah musuh, memberikan bonus seperempat; dan ketika dia kembali dan semua orang bersama, beliau memberikan bonus sepertiga.

1755.iwh_en: It was narrated from Habib ibn Maslamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ granted a bonus of one third after the khums (one fifth).
1755.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Habib ibn Maslamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ memberikan bonus sepertiga setelah khums (seperlima).

1756.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a disbeliever, he gets his spoils.' On that day Abu Talhah killed twenty and took their spoils.
1756.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh orang kafir, dia mendapatkan rampasannya.' Pada hari itu Abu Thalhah membunuh dua puluh orang dan mengambil rampasan mereka.

1757.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I dueled a man and killed him, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me his spoils.'
1757.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku bertarung satu lawan satu dengan seorang laki-laki dan membunuhnya, dan Rasulullah ﷺ memberiku rampasannya.'

1758.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ disliked giving extra spoils to individuals and said: 'Let the strong among the Muslims give back to the weak among them.'
1758.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak menyukai pemberian rampasan ekstra kepada individu dan bersabda: 'Hendaklah yang kuat di antara kaum Muslimin mengembalikan kepada yang lemah di antara mereka.'

1759.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to say: 'Return even the thread and the needle, and beware of unlawful acquisition from the spoils, for it is a disgrace for its doer on the Day of Resurrection.'
1759.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa bersabda: 'Kembalikan bahkan benang dan jarum, dan waspadalah terhadap pengambilan harta secara tidak sah dari rampasan, karena itu adalah kehinaan bagi pelakunya pada Hari Kiamat.'

1760.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ruwayfi' ibn Thabit al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him not ride a beast from the spoils of the Muslims until it becomes worn out, then return it; and whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him not wear a garment from the spoils of the Muslims until it is worn out, then return it to the spoils.'
1760.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ruwayfi' ibn Tsabit al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, jangan menunggang hewan dari rampasan kaum Muslimin hingga habis dan kembalikan; dan barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, jangan memakai pakaian dari rampasan kaum Muslimin hingga habis, lalu kembalikan ke rampasan.'

1761.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: Some men were killed on the day of Khaybar, and people said: So-and-so is a martyr. But the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No indeed! I saw him in Hellfire in a cloak he had taken unlawfully.' And he said: 'Get up, O Umar, and call out to the people that only the believers will enter Paradise.' So I went out and called out that only the believers will enter Paradise.
1761.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Beberapa orang terbunuh pada hari Khaibar, dan orang-orang berkata: Si fulan adalah syahid. Namun Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak sama sekali! Aku melihatnya di neraka dalam jubah yang dia ambil secara tidak sah.' Dan beliau bersabda: 'Berdirilah, wahai Umar, dan umumkan kepada orang-orang bahwa hanya orang-orang beriman yang akan masuk surga.' Maka aku keluar dan mengumumkan bahwa hanya orang-orang beriman yang akan masuk surga.

1762.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not meet the enemy wishing for victory with false witnesses; but if you meet them, be patient.'
1762.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan menemui musuh sambil mengharapkan kemenangan dengan saksi-saksi palsu; tetapi jika kamu menemui mereka, bersabarlah.'

1763.iwh_en: It was narrated from Salman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Ribat (guarding the frontier) for one day and one night is better than fasting and night prayer for a full month, and if he dies he will be rewarded for his deeds and given sustenance.'
1763.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Salman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ribath (menjaga perbatasan) sehari semalam lebih baik dari puasa dan shalat malam selama sebulan penuh, dan jika dia meninggal dia akan diberi pahala atas amal-amalnya dan diberi rezeki.'

1764.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Guarding the frontier for one day in the cause of Allah is better than the world and all it contains.'
1764.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Sa'd al-Sa'idi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Menjaga perbatasan satu hari di jalan Allah lebih baik dari dunia dan segala isinya.'

1765.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever dies and has not participated in a campaign nor equipped a campaigner, Allah will afflict him with some calamity before he dies.'
1765.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa meninggal dan tidak pernah berpartisipasi dalam suatu ekspedisi maupun membekali seorang pejuang, Allah akan menimpakan suatu bencana kepadanya sebelum dia meninggal.'

1766.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever sets out in the cause of Allah and does not depart until he is pleased with his provision and mount — every day and night he spends in travel counts for him as a hundred years of worship and martyrdom.'
1766.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berangkat di jalan Allah dan tidak pergi sampai dia puas dengan bekal dan tungganggannya — setiap hari dan malam yang dia habiskan dalam perjalanan terhitung baginya seperti seratus tahun ibadah dan kesyahidan.'

1767.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Shall I not inform you of the best of the dead? A man who expends himself and his horse in the cause of Allah until death comes to him while he is facing forward, neither turning his back.'
1767.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Maukah aku beritahu kalian tentang sebaik-baik orang yang meninggal? Seorang laki-laki yang menghabiskan dirinya dan kudanya di jalan Allah hingga kematian datang kepadanya sementara dia menghadap ke depan, tidak berbalik.'

1768.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The best jihad is the one whose horse is hamstrung and whose blood is shed.'
1768.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jihad terbaik adalah yang kudanya dipotong ototnya dan darahnya tertumpah.'

1769.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to make istigfar (seek forgiveness) for the martyrs of Uhud every year. He would go to them and say: Peace be upon you for your patience, how excellent the final abode.'
1769.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa memohonkan ampunan bagi para syuhada Uhud setiap tahun. Beliau pergi menemui mereka dan bersabda: 'Salam sejahtera atas kalian karena kesabaran kalian, sungguh indah abode akhir.'

1770.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a ring made of iron, then he made a ring of silver, on which was engraved 'Muhammad Messenger of Allah', and said: 'Let no one engrave like my engraving.'
1770.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memiliki cincin besi, kemudian beliau membuat cincin perak, yang di atasnya terukir 'Muhammad Rasul Allah', dan bersabda: 'Jangan ada seorang pun yang mengukir seperti ukiranku.'

1771.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ wrote to Heraclius, the Chosroes, and the Negus, inviting them to Islam. He was told: They will not accept your letter unless it has a seal. So he had a ring made of silver engraved with 'Muhammad Messenger of Allah'.
1771.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menulis kepada Heraklius, Kisra, dan Najasyi, mengundang mereka kepada Islam. Beliau diberitahu: Mereka tidak akan menerima suratmu kecuali memiliki segel. Maka beliau membuat cincin perak yang terukir 'Muhammad Rasul Allah'.

1772.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent a letter to the ruler of Bahrain inviting him to Islam, and the ruler of Bahrain embraced Islam.
1772.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengirim surat kepada penguasa Bahrain mengundangnya kepada Islam, dan penguasa Bahrain memeluk Islam.

1773.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered the Ka'bah and saw images, and said: 'O Ali, go to your father, Abu Talib, and bring water.' Then water was brought and the images were erased.
1773.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk ke Ka'bah dan melihat gambar-gambar, dan bersabda: 'Wahai Ali, pergi ke ayahmu, Abu Thalib, dan bawa air.' Kemudian air dibawa dan gambar-gambar itu dihapus.

1774.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered Mecca on the day of the Conquest and around the Ka'bah were three hundred and sixty idols. He began jabbing them with a stick in his hand, saying: 'Truth has come and falsehood has vanished; indeed falsehood is ever-vanishing.' [Quran 17:81]
1774.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk ke Mekah pada hari Fathu Makkah dan di sekitar Ka'bah terdapat tiga ratus enam puluh berhala. Beliau mulai menusuk-nusuk mereka dengan tongkat di tangannya, sambil bersabda: 'Kebenaran telah datang dan kebatilan telah lenyap; sesungguhnya kebatilan itu pasti lenyap.' [QS. Al-Isra: 81]

1775.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, on the day of the Conquest of Mecca, said: 'There is no emigration after the conquest, but jihad and intention. And when you are called to go out, then go out.'
1775.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, pada hari Fathu Makkah, bersabda: 'Tidak ada hijrah setelah penaklukan, tetapi ada jihad dan niat. Dan ketika kamu dipanggil untuk berangkat, maka berangkatlah.'

1776.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muawiyah ibn Abu Sufyan (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Hijra will not cease as long as the enemy is fought; and the Hijra will not cease as long as repentance is accepted; and repentance will not cease to be accepted until the sun rises from the west.'
1776.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Hijrah tidak akan berhenti selama musuh diperangi; dan hijrah tidak akan berhenti selama taubat diterima; dan taubat tidak akan berhenti diterima hingga matahari terbit dari barat.'

1777.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No two qiblas shall be observed in one land, and jizya shall not be imposed on a Muslim.'
1777.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dua kiblat tidak boleh dijalankan di satu negeri, dan jizyah tidak boleh dikenakan kepada seorang Muslim.'

1778.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A Muslim should not be killed for a disbeliever.'
1778.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang Muslim tidak boleh dibunuh karena membunuh orang kafir.'

1779.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Ansar are my inner circle and my confidants. People will increase in number but the Ansar will decrease. Accept the good deeds of the best among them and overlook the faults of their wrongdoers.'
1779.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kaum Anshar adalah orang dalam dan kepercayaanku. Manusia akan bertambah jumlahnya tetapi Anshar akan berkurang. Terimalah amal baik dari yang terbaik di antara mereka dan abaikan kesalahan orang-orang jahat di antara mereka.'

1780.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to the Ansar: 'You will find people who prefer themselves over you — be patient until you meet me, and your meeting place will be the Hawd.'
1780.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepada kaum Anshar: 'Kalian akan menemukan orang-orang yang lebih mengutamakan diri mereka sendiri daripada kalian — bersabarlah hingga kalian bertemu denganku, dan tempat pertemuan kalian adalah al-Hawd.'

1781.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Ansar are my inner circle. If people followed a valley or a mountain pass, I would follow the valley of the Ansar.'
1781.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kaum Anshar adalah orang dalam saya. Jika orang-orang mengikuti suatu lembah atau celah gunung, aku akan mengikuti lembah kaum Anshar.'

1782.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The sign of faith is love for the Ansar, and the sign of hypocrisy is hatred for the Ansar.'
1782.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tanda keimanan adalah mencintai kaum Anshar, dan tanda kemunafikan adalah membenci kaum Anshar.'

1783.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Ansar — love them and you will prosper, and disrespect them and you will perish.'
1783.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kaum Anshar — cintailah mereka maka kamu akan beruntung, dan hinakan mereka maka kamu akan binasa.'

1784.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever harms the Ansar, Allah will harm him; and whoever loves the Ansar, Allah will love him.'
1784.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menyakiti kaum Anshar, Allah akan menyakitinya; dan barangsiapa mencintai kaum Anshar, Allah akan mencintainya.'

1785.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I charge you with the Ansar, for they are my most trusted, my closest, and those whom I have drawn near. You will find after me that people will expand in number, but they will diminish. So accept the good of those who do good among them and forgive those who commit wrong among them.'
1785.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku mewasiatkan kepada kalian tentang kaum Anshar, karena mereka adalah yang paling dipercaya, yang paling dekat, dan yang telah aku dekatkan. Kalian akan mendapati setelahku bahwa manusia akan bertambah jumlahnya, namun mereka akan berkurang. Maka terimalah kebaikan dari mereka yang berbuat baik di antara mereka dan maafkan mereka yang berbuat salah di antara mereka.'

1786.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The people of the tribe of so-and-so are not my allies. My allies are the God-fearing, whoever they may be.' He said that three times.
1786.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang-orang dari kabilah si fulan bukanlah sekutu-sekutuku. Sekutu-sekutuku adalah orang-orang bertakwa, siapa pun mereka.' Beliau mengatakannya tiga kali.

1787.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Quraysh, the Ansar, the tribe of Juhaynah, the tribe of Muzaynah, Aslam, and Ghifar — these are my allies, with no ally beyond Allah and His Messenger.'
1787.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Quraisy, kaum Anshar, suku Juhainah, suku Muzainah, Aslam, dan Ghifar — mereka adalah sekutu-sekutuku, tidak ada sekutu selain Allah dan Rasul-Nya.'

1788.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jubair ibn Mut'im (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding the captives of Badr: 'If al-Mutim ibn Adi were alive and had interceded for these filthy ones, I would have released them for his sake.'
1788.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jubair ibn Mut'im (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda mengenai tawanan Badar: 'Kalau al-Muth'im ibn 'Adi masih hidup dan memohonkan pembebasan bagi orang-orang hina ini, niscaya aku akan membebaskan mereka demi dia.'

1789.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that Umar ibn al-Khattab said: 'Do not impute lineage except to the father on whose bed the child was born, and do not claim relationship with other than your own father, for it is disbelief in the blessings Allah gave your father and ingratitude, and whoever does not know his own father, then his father is what the people agreed on.'
1789.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Umar ibn al-Khattab berkata: 'Jangan menisbatkan nasab kecuali kepada ayah yang atas ranjangnya anak dilahirkan, dan jangan mengklaim hubungan dengan selain ayahmu sendiri, karena itu adalah mengingkari nikmat yang Allah berikan kepada ayahmu dan kufur nikmat, dan barangsiapa tidak mengetahui ayahnya sendiri, maka ayahnya adalah apa yang disepakati orang-orang.'

1790.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wathilah ibn al-Asqa' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Verily among the greatest of slanders is a man claiming to be from other than his true father or claiming to be in a tribe he does not belong to, or attributing to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ what he did not say.'
1790.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Watsilah ibn al-Asqa' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya termasuk kebohongan terbesar adalah seorang laki-laki yang mengklaim berasal dari selain ayah kandungnya, atau mengklaim berasal dari suku yang bukan miliknya, atau menisbatkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ apa yang tidak beliau sabdakan.'

1791.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not disavow your fathers, for whoever disavows his father has committed disbelief.'
1791.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan melepaskan diri dari ayah-ayah kalian, karena barangsiapa melepaskan diri dari ayahnya telah berbuat kufur.'

1792.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever claims a father other than his own father, knowing that it is other, Paradise is forbidden to him.'
1792.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengklaim ayah selain ayahnya sendiri, padahal mengetahui bahwa itu bukan, maka surga diharamkan baginya.'

1793.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The allies of the tribe are of the tribe.'
1793.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sekutu-sekutu suatu kabilah adalah bagian dari kabilah itu.'

1794.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Lineage is an ancestry, and alliance is an alliance; so the mawla (client) of a people is one of them, and the son of the sister of a people is one of them.'
1794.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Nasab adalah garis keturunan, dan perjanjian perlindungan adalah perjanjian perlindungan; maka mawla (klien) suatu kaum adalah bagian dari mereka, dan anak dari saudara perempuan suatu kaum adalah bagian dari mereka.'

1795.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The client (mawla) of a people belongs to them; he inherits from them and they inherit from him.'
1795.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Klien (mawla) suatu kaum adalah milik mereka; dia mewarisi dari mereka dan mereka mewarisi darinya.'

1796.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muawiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Every nation has one who is most impure, and the most impure of this nation is whoever claims ancestry to a tribe he does not belong to.'
1796.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Setiap umat memiliki orang yang paling hina, dan yang paling hina dari umat ini adalah siapa yang mengklaim keturunan kepada suku yang bukan miliknya.'

1797.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not resell what you have bought until you have taken full possession of it.'
1797.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan menjual kembali apa yang telah kamu beli sampai kamu benar-benar mengambil alih kepemilikannya.'

1798.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The closest people to me on the Day of Resurrection are those who prayed the most blessings upon me.'
1798.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang paling dekat denganku pada Hari Kiamat adalah mereka yang paling banyak bershalawat kepadaku.'

1799.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not make your houses graves, and do not make my grave a place of festival. Send blessings upon me, for your blessings reach me wherever you are.'
1799.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan jadikan rumah-rumah kalian sebagai kuburan, dan jangan jadikan kuburanku sebagai tempat perayaan. Ucapkan shalawat untukku, karena shalawat kalian akan sampai kepadaku di mana pun kalian berada.'

1800.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no person who sends blessings upon me but the angels bless him ten times in return.'
1800.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seseorang mengucapkan shalawat untukku melainkan para malaikat memohonkan sepuluh rahmat untuknya sebagai balasan.'

1801.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever sends blessings upon me once, Allah sends blessings upon him ten times, erases ten of his sins, and raises him ten degrees.'
1801.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mengucapkan shalawat untukku satu kali, Allah memohonkan sepuluh rahmat untuknya, menghapus sepuluh dosanya, dan mengangkatnya sepuluh derajat.'
